en_definition: the process of finding the size, amount, or degree of something
example1: Accurate measurement is essential in scientific research.
example1_ru: Точное измерение имеет огромное значение в научных исследованиях.
example2: The measurement of temperature requires a reliable thermometer.

en_definition: a single stage within a formal set of instructions or actions
example1: Skipping a protocol step can invalidate the entire experiment.
example1_ru: Пропуск одного этапа протокола может сделать весь эксперимент недействительным.
example2: Each protocol step must be recorded carefully in the lab notebook.

en_definition: equipment or machines used for a particular purpose
example1: The laboratory apparatus must be cleaned after each experiment.
example1_ru: Лабораторное оборудование необходимо очищать после каждого эксперимента.
example2: We assembled the apparatus according to the diagram.

en_definition: a material or matter of a particular kind
example1: This unknown substance reacts with acid in the experiment.
example1_ru: Это неизвестное вещество вступает в реакцию с кислотой в ходе эксперимента.
example2: The chemical substance was identified through testing.

en_definition: a number that is close to the exact amount but not completely precise
example1: The scale gave an approximate value for the sample's weight.
example1_ru: Весы дали приближённое значение массы образца.
example2: Students calculated an approximate value before checking the exact result.

en_definition: close to the actual number or amount, but not exact
example1: The approximate temperature of the solution is thirty degrees.
example1_ru: Приблизительная температура раствора составляет тридцать градусов.
example2: We need an approximate date for the project completion.

en_definition: exact and accurate in every detail
example1: Precise measurements are needed for this chemistry experiment.
example1_ru: Для этого химического опыта требуются точные измерения.
example2: The professor gave precise instructions for the lab work.

en_definition: correct and free from mistakes or errors
example1: Accurate data collection is critical for the research.
example1_ru: Точный сбор данных крайне важен для исследования.
example2: The student provided an accurate answer to the question.

en_definition: to examine collected information carefully to check for accuracy or patterns
example1: The team had to scrutinize data before publishing the results.
example1_ru: Команде пришлось тщательно изучить данные перед публикацией результатов.
example2: Reviewers scrutinize data closely before accepting the paper.

en_definition: to organize and perform an activity or experiment
example1: The professor conducted the experiment in front of the class.
example1_ru: Профессор провёл эксперимент перед классом.
example2: Researchers conduct surveys to gather information from people.

en_definition: to write down or document information for later use
example1: Record all the temperature readings in your notebook.
example1_ru: Запишите все показания температуры в свой блокнот.
example2: The assistant recorded the exact time of each observation.

en_definition: a small part or piece of something taken for testing
example1: The soil sample was analyzed for chemical composition.
example1_ru: Образец почвы был проанализирован на химический состав.
example2: We collected water samples from different locations.

en_definition: a change, often repeated, in the level or amount of something
example1: The graph shows a clear fluctuation in temperature over the week.
example1_ru: График показывает явное колебание температуры в течение недели.
example2: Scientists studied the fluctuation in pressure inside the container.

en_definition: a relationship between two amounts showing how many times one contains the other
example1: The recipe uses a two-to-one ratio of flour to sugar.
example1_ru: В рецепте используется соотношение муки и сахара два к одному.
example2: Researchers measured the ratio of oxygen to nitrogen in the sample.

en_definition: to match or agree closely with another set of results or expectations
example1: The new data align with earlier predictions from the model.
example1_ru: Новые данные соответствуют более ранним прогнозам модели.
example2: Her findings align with results published in an earlier study.

en_definition: to change something from one form or system to another
example1: Convert the temperature from Celsius to Fahrenheit.
example1_ru: Переведите температуру из градусов Цельсия в градусы Фаренгейта.
example2: You need to convert milliliters into liters for the calculation.

en_definition: to state or describe something in detail and clearly
example1: Please specify which apparatus you used in your experiment.
example1_ru: Пожалуйста, уточните, какое оборудование вы использовали в своём эксперименте.
example2: The instructions specify that you must wear protective glasses.

en_definition: to check information against another source to confirm it is correct
example1: Always cross-check your results with a second measurement.
example1_ru: Всегда перепроверяйте свои результаты повторным измерением.
example2: The editor will cross-check every fact before publishing the article.

en_definition: to explain something clearly and in detail, leaving nothing unclear
example1: The manual does not spell out how often to replace the filter.
example1_ru: В руководстве не разъясняется подробно, как часто нужно менять фильтр.
example2: Please spell out the exact date and time for the appointment.

en_definition: to give reasons or evidence to explain why something is right
example1: Justify your choice of methodology in the introduction section.
example1_ru: Обоснуйте выбор методологии во вводной части.
example2: You must justify why this procedure is better than alternatives.

en_definition: to make a quick simple drawing to show the main features
example1: Sketch the apparatus used in the experiment on your paper.
example1_ru: Сделайте эскиз оборудования, использованного в эксперименте, на своём листе.
example2: She sketched the crystal structure to illustrate the concept.

ru_translation: в свете, учитывая, принимая во внимание
en_definition: considering a particular fact or piece of information
example1: In light of the weather, the schedule was adjusted quickly.
example1_ru: С учётом погоды расписание было оперативно скорректировано.
example2: In light of the unclear rules, staff acted with extra caution.

en_definition: in spite of something; without being affected by a particular fact
example1: Notwithstanding the delays, the project finished within budget.
example1_ru: Несмотря на задержки, проект был завершён в рамках бюджета.
example2: Her illness notwithstanding, she completed the entire marathon.

en_definition: in the order just mentioned, one after another
example1: The first and second groups showed improvements of five and ten percent, respectively.
example1_ru: Первая и вторая группы показали улучшение на пять и десять процентов соответственно.
example2: Elements A, B, and C have atomic numbers six, seven, and eight, respectively.

en_definition: to assume something is true before continuing an argument or plan
example1: The theory presuppose that all participants act rationally.
example1_ru: Теория заранее предполагает, что все участники действуют рационально.
example2: Good instructions should not presuppose prior technical knowledge.

en_definition: to use in mathematical or scientific notation to establish a value
example1: Let x be the unknown variable in this equation.
example1_ru: Пусть x будет неизвестной переменной в этом уравнении.
example2: Let the initial volume be measured in milliliters.

en_definition: pay attention to an important point or observation
example1: Note that the experiment must be conducted at room temperature.
example1_ru: Обратите внимание, что эксперимент должен проводиться при комнатной температуре.
example2: Note that all values should be recorded to three decimal places.

en_definition: on the condition that something is true or happens
example1: The experiment will succeed provided that the pH remains constant.
example1_ru: Эксперимент увенчается успехом при условии, что pH останется постоянным.
example2: Funding will be available provided that the project meets requirements.

en_definition: to find or identify the exact location or cause of something
example1: Engineers managed to pinpoint the source of the electrical fault.
example1_ru: Инженерам удалось точно определить источник электрической неисправности.
example2: Doctors used scans to pinpoint the exact site of the injury.

en_definition: to examine something in great detail, or to cut something apart to study it
example1: Students dissect a frog during the biology practical class.
example1_ru: Студенты препарируют лягушку на практическом занятии по биологии.
example2: Critics dissect every scene of the director's newest film.

en_definition: a physical or chemical change that occurs when substances interact
example1: The chemical reaction produced a yellow precipitate.
example1_ru: Химическая реакция дала жёлтый осадок.
example2: A reaction began immediately when we mixed the two solutions.

en_definition: a quantity in an equation or experiment that does not change
example1: Gravity is treated as a constant value in this equation.
example1_ru: В этом уравнении сила тяжести рассматривается как постоянная величина.
example2: The experiment kept temperature as a constant value throughout.

en_definition: facts or information that show something is true
example1: The detective needed solid proof before making an arrest.
example1_ru: Детективу требовались веские доказательства, прежде чем производить арест.
example2: There is little proof to support the company's bold claims.

en_definition: to successfully arrange money needed for a project or organization
example1: The lab hoped to secure funding before the semester began.
example1_ru: Лаборатория надеялась получить финансирование до начала семестра.
example2: Small startups often struggle to secure funding early on.

en_definition: the speed at which something happens or changes
example1: The reaction rate increased with higher temperature.
example1_ru: Скорость реакции увеличивалась с повышением температуры.
example2: The growth rate of the bacteria was measured per hour.

en_definition: the amount of a substance in a given volume of liquid or mixture
example1: The concentration of the acid solution was measured in moles per liter.
example1_ru: Концентрация раствора кислоты измерялась в молях на литр.
example2: Higher concentration of oxygen affected the combustion rate.

en_definition: to cause a solid substance to become liquid or solution
example1: Dissolve the salt in hot water until it disappears.
example1_ru: Растворите соль в горячей воде, пока она полностью не исчезнет.
example2: The sugar will dissolve slowly in cold liquid.

en_definition: to move something from one place or container to another
example1: Transfer the solution carefully to the graduated cylinder.
example1_ru: Осторожно перелейте раствор в мерный цилиндр.
example2: The heat was transferred through the metal rod.

en_definition: to do something again in the same way
example1: Repeat the experiment three times to ensure reliable results.
example1_ru: Повторите эксперимент трижды, чтобы обеспечить надёжность результатов.
example2: Please repeat your answer so everyone can hear.

en_definition: deserving of trust because of being honest and dependable
example1: The witness was considered a trustworthy source by the police.
example1_ru: Полиция сочла свидетеля заслуживающим доверия источником.
example2: Only a trustworthy method should be used to test new medicine.

en_definition: based on good reasoning and evidence, or legally acceptable
example1: Your methodology is valid for testing the hypothesis.
example1_ru: Ваша методология подходит для проверки гипотезы.
example2: A valid experiment must have proper controls.

en_definition: a difference between an expected value and the actual measured value
example1: The report noted a small deviation from the expected results.
example1_ru: В отчёте отмечено небольшое отклонение от ожидаемых результатов.
example2: Any large deviation in the readings must be investigated immediately.

en_definition: an amount of space or allowance allowed for error or variation
example1: The measurement has a margin of error of plus or minus one unit.
example1_ru: Погрешность измерения составляет плюс-минус одну единицу.
example2: We must stay within the safety margin.

en_definition: the highest limit a measurement or quantity is allowed to reach
example1: The device shuts down automatically above its ceiling value.
example1_ru: Устройство автоматически отключается при превышении предельного значения.
example2: Regulators set a ceiling value for allowed emissions.

en_definition: to adjust an instrument so that it gives accurate measurements
example1: Calibrate the scale before weighing your samples.
example1_ru: Откалибруйте весы перед взвешиванием образцов.
example2: The thermometer must be calibrated before use.

en_definition: to measure or express something as a number or quantity
example1: Quantify the amount of substance produced in the reaction.
example1_ru: Измерьте количественно вещество, образовавшееся в реакции.
example2: It is hard to quantify the exact impact of the change.

en_definition: to regularly check and record how something is developing over time
example1: The app lets users track progress toward their fitness goals.
example1_ru: Приложение позволяет пользователям отслеживать прогресс в достижении фитнес-целей.
example2: Teachers track progress using weekly quizzes and reports.

en_definition: a series of marks indicating measurements or degrees on an instrument
example1: Read the temperature from the scale on the thermometer.
example1_ru: Считайте температуру по шкале термометра.
example2: The pH scale ranges from zero to fourteen.

en_definition: a standard amount used for measuring something
example1: Express your answer in SI units for the report.
example1_ru: Выразите свой ответ в единицах СИ в отчёте.
example2: The unit of mass in this system is the kilogram.

en_definition: the quality of being very exact and accurate
example1: High precision instruments are needed for this experiment.
example1_ru: Для этого эксперимента нужны высокоточные приборы.
example2: The precision of measurements affects the validity of results.

en_definition: the quality of being correct and without errors
example1: The accuracy of our data depends on the instrument quality.
example1_ru: Точность наших данных зависит от качества прибора.
example2: We must ensure accuracy when recording all measurements.

en_definition: a standard indicating how advanced or difficult material is meant to be
example1: The textbook is written for an intermediate grade level.
example1_ru: Учебник написан для среднего уровня сложности.
example2: Teachers adjust lessons to match each student's grade level.

en_definition: how quickly something increases or decreases over time
example1: The rate of change of temperature was two degrees per minute.
example1_ru: Скорость изменения температуры составляла два градуса в минуту.
example2: Calculate the rate of change in the population growth.

en_definition: a general direction or tendency in which something is changing
example1: The data shows an upward trend in temperature.
example1_ru: Данные показывают восходящую тенденцию температуры.
example2: We observed a clear trend in the student performance.

en_definition: a data point that differs greatly from other values in a set
example1: The scientist removed one outlier before calculating the average.
example1_ru: Учёный удалил одно аномальное значение перед расчётом среднего.
example2: A single outlier skewed the results of the entire survey.

en_definition: a relationship in which one thing relies on or is affected by another
example1: The software has a dependency on an outdated library.
example1_ru: В программном обеспечении есть зависимость от устаревшей библиотеки.
example2: Researchers examined the dependency between rainfall and crop yield.

en_definition: the way something is spread out or arranged
example1: The distribution of the particles was uneven in the solution.
example1_ru: Распределение частиц в растворе было неравномерным.
example2: Study the distribution of values in the frequency table.

en_definition: the length of time or distance that something covers
example1: The bridge has a span of nearly two kilometers.
example1_ru: Протяжённость моста составляет почти два километра.
example2: Researchers studied the effects over a ten-year span.

en_definition: the limits within which something varies or can exist
example1: The temperature range for this experiment is ten to thirty degrees.
example1_ru: Диапазон температур для этого эксперимента составляет от десяти до тридцати градусов.
example2: The price range for textbooks varies between fifty and two hundred.

en_definition: a gradual change in a value from one point to another
example1: The temperature gradient across the rod was measured precisely.
example1_ru: Температурный градиент вдоль стержня был точно измерен.
example2: A steep gradient indicates rapid change in values.

en_definition: the limit at which something begins to happen or change
example1: The threshold value for alarm activation was reached at noon.
example1_ru: Пороговое значение для срабатывания сигнализации было достигнуто в полдень.
example2: Set the threshold value carefully before starting the measurement.

en_definition: the most favorable or best condition for something to happen
example1: The optimum temperature for enzyme activity is thirty-seven degrees.
example1_ru: Оптимальная температура для активности фермента составляет тридцать семь градусов.
example2: Find the optimum conditions for maximum plant growth.

en_definition: to control an experiment so that only one factor changes at a time
example1: Good experiments isolate a variable to test its exact effect.
example1_ru: В хороших экспериментах выделяют одну переменную, чтобы проверить именно её эффект.
example2: Researchers isolate a variable before drawing final conclusions.

en_definition: to separate or set apart a substance or organism from others
example1: Isolate the pure compound from the mixture using filtration.
example1_ru: Выделите чистое соединение из смеси с помощью фильтрации.
example2: We need to isolate the virus to study its properties.

en_definition: to remove unwanted substances from something to make it pure
example1: Purify the water by distillation to remove all impurities.
example1_ru: Очистите воду с помощью дистилляции, чтобы удалить все примеси.
example2: The process will purify the crystal to make it clean.

en_definition: to remove particles or unwanted material using a strainer
example1: Filter the mixture to separate the solid from the liquid.
example1_ru: Отфильтруйте смесь, чтобы отделить твёрдое вещество от жидкости.
example2: We filtered the solution through fine paper.

en_definition: to spin a mixture rapidly to separate components by density
example1: Centrifuge the blood sample to separate the cells from serum.
example1_ru: Центрифугируйте образец крови, чтобы отделить клетки от сыворотки.
example2: You must centrifuge the mixture at high speed for five minutes.

en_definition: to change from liquid into gas due to heating
example1: Evaporate the water to obtain the solid salt crystals.
example1_ru: Выпарите воду, чтобы получить твёрдые кристаллы соли.
example2: The liquid evaporated slowly at room temperature.

en_definition: to reduce the size of digital information so it takes up less space
example1: The app can compress data before sending it over the network.
example1_ru: Приложение может сжимать данные перед отправкой по сети.
example2: Engineers compress data to save storage space on the server.

en_definition: to form crystals from a solution as it cools or dries
example1: Allow the salt solution to crystallize overnight in the lab.
example1_ru: Дайте раствору соли кристаллизоваться в лаборатории в течение ночи.
example2: The minerals crystallize as the liquid evaporates slowly.

en_definition: a solid substance that forms when a gas or liquid changes state
example1: A white precipitate formed when the chemicals were mixed.
example1_ru: При смешивании химических веществ образовался белый осадок.
example2: The precipitate was filtered and dried carefully.

en_definition: a remaining substance after a process has been completed
example1: The residue at the bottom must be examined under a microscope.
example1_ru: Осадок на дне необходимо изучить под микроскопом.
example2: Burn the residue and measure the ash content.

en_definition: a substance dissolved in a liquid during the making of a solution
example1: The solute dissolved completely in the warm water.
example1_ru: Растворённое вещество полностью растворилось в тёплой воде.
example2: Identify the solute in this unknown solution.

en_definition: a liquid that dissolves another substance to make a solution
example1: Water is the most common solvent used in chemistry labs.
example1_ru: Вода — самый распространённый растворитель, используемый в химических лабораториях.
example2: Choose the solvent based on what substance you need to dissolve.

en_definition: a chemical substance used in a reaction to produce a change
example1: Add the reagent drop by drop to the test tube.
example1_ru: Добавляйте реактив по каплям в пробирку.
example2: The reagent will change color when the test is positive.

en_definition: an event or substance that causes a particular reaction to begin
example1: Pollen is a common trigger for seasonal allergies in spring.
example1_ru: Пыльца — распространённый триггер сезонной аллергии весной.
example2: The alarm has a sensitive trigger that reacts to smoke.

en_definition: a scale measuring how acidic or alkaline a substance is
example1: Measure the pH of the solution using pH paper.
example1_ru: Измерьте pH раствора с помощью индикаторной бумаги.
example2: A pH of seven indicates a neutral solution exactly.

en_definition: the number of moles of a substance in one liter of solution
example1: Calculate the molarity of the solution using the formula.
example1_ru: Рассчитайте молярность раствора по формуле.
example2: The molarity affects how the solution reacts with other compounds.

en_definition: a chemical process where a substance loses electrons to oxygen
example1: Oxidation of iron produces rust on the metal surface.
example1_ru: Окисление железа приводит к образованию ржавчины на поверхности металла.
example2: The oxidation reaction released heat and light.

en_definition: a chemical process where a substance gains electrons
example1: Reduction of copper oxide produces pure copper metal.
example1_ru: Восстановление оксида меди даёт чистый металлический медь.
example2: The reduction reaction requires heat to proceed forward.

en_definition: describing a reaction that releases heat into the surroundings
example1: Combustion is an exothermic reaction that produces flame.
example1_ru: Горение — экзотермическая реакция, сопровождающаяся образованием пламени.
example2: The exothermic process caused the temperature to rise rapidly.

en_definition: describing a reaction that absorbs heat from the surroundings
example1: Melting ice is an endothermic process that requires heat.
example1_ru: Плавление льда — эндотермический процесс, требующий притока тепла.
example2: The endothermic reaction made the container feel cold.

en_definition: a state where opposing forces or reactions are balanced
example1: The system reached equilibrium when the reaction rates became equal.
example1_ru: Система достигла равновесия, когда скорости реакций стали равными.
example2: Disturbing equilibrium causes the system to respond.

en_definition: the state when no more of a substance can be dissolved or added
example1: The solution reached saturation when no more salt dissolved.
example1_ru: Раствор достиг насыщения, когда соль перестала растворяться.
example2: High saturation of the atmosphere causes condensation.

en_definition: to keep something floating or in the middle of a liquid
example1: Suspend the particles in water to create a uniform mixture.
example1_ru: Взвесьте частицы в воде, чтобы получить однородную смесь.
example2: The clay particles will suspend in water temporarily.

en_definition: solid material that settles at the bottom of a liquid
example1: The sediment settled to the bottom after several hours.
example1_ru: Осадок осел на дно через несколько часов.
example2: Examine the sediment under the microscope carefully.

en_definition: how thick and sticky a liquid is, affecting how easily it flows
example1: The viscosity of oil is much higher than water.
example1_ru: Вязкость масла значительно выше, чем у воды.
example2: Temperature affects the viscosity of the solution.

en_definition: the mass of a substance divided by its volume
example1: Calculate the density of the unknown metal by measurement.
example1_ru: Рассчитайте плотность неизвестного металла путём измерений.
example2: Water has a density of one gram per cubic centimeter.

en_definition: the amount of matter in an object, measured in kilograms
example1: Measure the mass of the sample using a balance carefully.
example1_ru: Тщательно измерьте массу образца с помощью весов.
example2: The mass remains constant regardless of location.

en_definition: the amount of space occupied by a substance or object
example1: Calculate the volume of the liquid using the graduated cylinder.
example1_ru: Рассчитайте объём жидкости с помощью мерного цилиндра.
example2: The volume of gas produced was measured at room temperature.

en_definition: a device that automatically controls temperature by turning heating on or off
example1: She lowered the thermostat before leaving for work in the morning.
example1_ru: Она понизила температуру на термостате перед уходом на работу утром.
example2: The old thermostat stopped working during the coldest week of winter.

en_definition: the force exerted on a surface per unit area
example1: Increase the pressure to speed up the reaction rate.
example1_ru: Увеличьте давление, чтобы ускорить скорость реакции.
example2: Atmospheric pressure affects boiling point of water.

en_definition: the distance between two consecutive points of a wave
example1: Light with shorter wavelength appears more violet in color.
example1_ru: Свет с более короткой длиной волны выглядит более фиолетовым.
example2: Measure the wavelength using the interference pattern.

en_definition: how many times something happens in a given time period
example1: The frequency of the sound wave affects how high it sounds.
example1_ru: Частота звуковой волны определяет высоту звука.
example2: Calculate the frequency of the oscillation per second.

en_definition: the maximum extent of movement in a wave or oscillation
example1: Increase the amplitude to make the vibration stronger.
example1_ru: Увеличьте амплитуду, чтобы усилить вибрацию.
example2: The amplitude of the seismic wave indicates earthquake strength.

en_definition: the speed of something in a particular direction
example1: Calculate the velocity of the object using distance and time.
example1_ru: Рассчитайте скорость объекта, используя расстояние и время.
example2: The velocity of light is constant in vacuum.

en_definition: the rate at which velocity increases or decreases
example1: The acceleration due to gravity is nine point eight meters per second squared.
example1_ru: Ускорение свободного падения составляет девять целых восемь десятых метра в секунду в квадрате.
example2: Calculate the acceleration of the moving object carefully.

en_definition: a physical influence that causes something to move or change
example1: Apply a constant force to move the object smoothly.
example1_ru: Приложите постоянную силу, чтобы плавно сдвинуть объект.
example2: Gravity is the force pulling objects toward earth.

en_definition: the capacity to do work or cause change in physical systems
example1: Calculate the energy released in the chemical reaction.
example1_ru: Рассчитайте энергию, выделившуюся в ходе химической реакции.
example2: The energy of the system must be conserved throughout.

en_definition: a statement assumed to be true and used as a basis for an argument
example1: The whole argument rests on a single questionable underlying premise.
example1_ru: Весь аргумент строится на одной сомнительной исходной предпосылке.
example2: If the underlying premise is false, the conclusion cannot be trusted.
